subject: Chemistry
solution:
$XS(s) \rightleftharpoons X^{+2}(aq.) + S^{2-}(aq.)$
For precipitation of $XS(s)$
$[X^{+2}][S^{2-}] \geq K_{sp}(XS)$
$[S^{2-}] \geq \dfrac{1 \times 10^{-22}}{0.01} = 10^{-20}$
$YS(s) \rightleftharpoons Y^{+2}(aq) + S^{2-}(aq)$
For precipitation of $YS(s)$
$[Y^{+2}][S^{2-}] \geq K_{sp}(YS)$
$[S^{2-}] \geq \dfrac{4 \times 10^{-16}}{10^{-2}} = 4 \times 10^{-14}$
Now, $H_2S(aq) \rightleftharpoons 2H^+(aq) + S^{2-}(aq)$
$\dfrac{[S^{2-}][H^+]^2}{H_2S} = K_{a_1} \times K_{a_2} = 1 \times 10^{-21}$
$[S^{2-}] = \dfrac{1 \times 10^{-21} \times [H_2S]}{[H^+]^2} \geq 4 \times 10^{-14}$
$[H^+]^2 \leq \dfrac{1}{4} \times 10^{-7} \times 10^{-1}$
$[H^+] \leq \dfrac{1}{2} \times 10^{-4} \Rightarrow pH \geq 4.3$
Nearest integer pH = 4.

solution: Methyl orange is a popular pH indicator that is used in the acid base titration.Unlike a universal indicator, methyl orange does not have a full spectrum of color change, but it has a sharp end point.The colour of the solution changes from yellow to red when methyl orange is used as an indicator.That means in acidic medium it is red and exists in quinonoid form

subject: Chemistry
solution:
When ionic product i.e. the product of the concentration of ions in the solution exceeds the value of solubility product, formation of precitpiate occurs.
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{CaF}_2 \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Ca}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{~F}^{-} \\ & \text {Ionic product }=\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2 \\ & \text { when, }\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]=1 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{M} \\ & {\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2=\left(1 \times 10^{-3}\right)^2 \mathrm{M} } \\ & =1 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{M} \\ \therefore & {\left[\mathrm{Ca}^{2+}\right]\left[\mathrm{F}^{-}\right]^2=\left(1 \times 10^{-2}\right)\left(1 \times 10^{-6}\right)=1 \times 10^{-8} } \\ & \text { In this case, } \\ & \text { Ionic product }\left(1 \times 10^{-8}\right)> \\ & \text { solubility product }\left(1.7 \times 10^{-10}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Hence (a) is correct option.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The molar solubility $S$ of the salt is given by $S = \dfrac{x}{y}$ mol L$^{-1}$.

The dissociation of the sparingly soluble salt $M_3A_2$ is:

$M_3A_2 \rightleftharpoons 3M^{2+} + 2A^{3-}$

The molar concentration of the anion $A^{3-}$ is:

$[A^{3-}] = 2S$

The solubility product $K_{sp}$ of the salt is:

$K_{sp} = [M^{2+}]^3 [A^{3-}]^2 = (3S)^3 (2S)^2 = 27S^3 \times 4S^2 = 108S^5$

The ratio of the molar concentration of the anion to the solubility product is:

$\dfrac{[A^{3-}]}{K_{sp}} = \dfrac{2S}{108S^5} = \dfrac{1}{54S^4}$

Substituting $S = \dfrac{x}{y}$ into the expression, we get:

$\text{Ratio} = \dfrac{1}{54 \left(\dfrac{x}{y}\right)^4} = \dfrac{1}{54} \cdot \dfrac{y^4}{x^4}$

Answer: $\dfrac{1}{54} \cdot \dfrac{y^4}{x^4}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Phenolphthalein is an organic acid and can be represented as HPh.

HPh (colourless) ⇌H++Ph- (Pink) 

Using Henderson equation for phenolphthalein

pH=pKIn+logPh-[HPh]

At equivalence point  Ph-=[HPh]

∴pH2=pKIn=-log[4]+10=-0.6+10=9.4

Hence at (9.4±1)PH , phenolphthalein starts changing colour. Phenolphthalein is colourless in acidic medium and pink in basic medium. Phenolphthalein indicator distinguish the pH change between 8 to 10. Therefore, it is used for strong acid and strong base titration or weak acid and strong base titration. Hence, A and C are incorrect statements.
